planted_bugs.marker: PLANTED BUG (T2): json resource missing mimeType
planted_bugs.description: The `cuisines` resource (registered via server.registerResource) returns JSON content but declares no mimeType, in either the registration metadata or the read callback's contents[] entry, so clients can't tell it isn't text/plain.

planted_bugs.marker: PLANTED BUG (T3a): template param/URI variable mismatch
planted_bugs.description: The `cuisine-recipes` resource template's URI is `recipes://cuisine/{cuisineSlug}`, but the read callback destructures `{ cuisine }`, so the parameter is always undefined and the filter never matches any recipe.

planted_bugs.marker: PLANTED BUG (T3b): complete map key mismatches URI variable, completions never enabled
planted_bugs.description: The `ingredient-info` resource template's `complete` map is keyed `ingredient`, but the URI template variable is `ingredientName`. The SDK's completion auto-detection only checks real URI template variable names, so it never finds a match, never enables the completion handler, and the server never declares the `completions` capability, even though the callback code exists.

planted_bugs.marker: PLANTED BUG (T4): single-call prompt that should be a tool
planted_bugs.description: The `recipe_detail_prompt` prompt wraps exactly one tool call (get_recipe_detail) with its `id` argument passed straight through and no chaining across multiple tools, so it should be a tool, not a prompt. Its description ('Prompt for recipe detail') is also vague and noun-first, the same class of weak description flagged on the tools.

planted_bugs.marker: PLANTED BUG (T5a): dotted tool name breaks Claude API
planted_bugs.description: The `recipes.search` tool (renamed from search_by_ingredient) uses a dot in its name. The MCP spec charset (A-Za-z0-9_.-) allows this, but the Claude API's tool-name validation charset ([a-zA-Z0-9_-]) does not, so the tool breaks on the largest MCP client even though it is spec-legal. Fix: rename to underscores, e.g. recipes_search.

No registered tool, resource, template, or prompt declares an `icons` array (SEP-973), and there is no icon.svg at the server's repo root for Smithery's icon category. Absence-type bug: there is nothing in existing code to rename or correct, the gap is that no icon metadata exists anywhere in the fixture.
